feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Community
feed.subtitle: SAP Community blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: #SITCPS2025 - 🗣️Soluções SAP para Transformação de Negócios
entry.content:
Palestrante: Rodrigo Cordeiro @Rodrigo_Cordeiro 

Bio: Rodrigo Cordeiro é um profissional com mais de 25 anos de experiência em áreas como Planejamento Estratégico, Gestão de Logística e Gestão de Cadeia de Suprimentos. Atuando atualmente com transformação de negócios, ele alia visão prática e otimismo em sua abordagem.

Título da Palestra

Soluções SAP para Transformação de Negócios

Breve Descrição da Palestra

Nesta palestra, Rodrigo apresenta os desafios enfrentados pelas organizações diante da necessidade de constante adaptação. Com foco em soluções SAP como Signavio, LeanIX e WalkME, ele demonstra como essas ferramentas contribuem para otimizar processos de forma estratégica e eficaz, viabilizando uma adaptação ágil e sustentável.

*** Link com todas as palestras ***

A transformação como necessidade vital

Rodrigo inicia destacando que a transformação de negócios não é mais uma opção, mas uma questão de sobrevivência. Inspirado por reflexões de Darwin e Leon Megginson, ele ressalta que a adaptabilidade é a chave no atual cenário corporativo volátil e imprevisível.

A complexidade do cenário atual

Rodrigo ilustra como o ambiente de negócios se tornou mais complexo com a digitalização e a multiplicidade de ferramentas e processos. A SAP responde a isso com a estrutura de Business Transformation Management (BTM), que organiza a transformação com foco em pessoas, processos, dados e aplicações.

LeanIX: mapeando o caminho estratégico

Apresentado como um “mapa”, o LeanIX atua na camada estratégica da arquitetura corporativa. A ferramenta organiza os ativos de TI, identifica lacunas e alinha tecnologia à estratégia. Rodrigo alerta sobre o risco de usá-la apenas como catálogo e não como ferramenta de transformação.

Signavio: da linguagem comum à melhoria contínua

Tratado como um “facilitador de comunicação”, o Signavio atua na camada tática. Ele ajuda a traduzir os processos do negócio para a linguagem tecnológica e vice-versa, construindo uma ponte entre diferentes áreas. Rodrigo explica como suas ferramentas (como o Process Insights e Process Intelligence) trazem dados reais e objetivos, viabilizando melhorias baseadas em fatos e não em opiniões.

WalkME: personal trainer digital

Na camada operacional, WalkME surge como uma solução de adoção digital. Comparado a um personal trainer, ele acompanha o usuário em tempo real durante o uso de sistemas SAP, oferecendo orientação e treinamentos contextuais. Rodrigo ressalta o ganho de eficiência e aderência proporcionado pela ferramenta.

Integração entre soluções

Rodrigo também demonstra como essas soluções se integram — por exemplo, o mapeamento feito no Signavio revela lacunas que são preenchidas com dados do LeanIX. Esse ecossistema integrado torna o processo de transformação mais coerente e eficaz.

Reflexão final

Ele conclui com uma citação de Peter Drucker: “as únicas coisas que evoluem por vontade própria em uma organização são desordem, atrito e mau desempenho”. Para ele, a transformação exige intenção, estratégia e ferramentas adequadas.

se você deseja ser notificado sobre os próximos eventos SAP Inside Track no Brasil, entre na nossa lista de interesse: Cadastre-se aqui para ser notificado

entry.title: #SITCPS2025 - 🗣️Joule: A Nova Queridinha da SAP
entry.content:
Nome do Palestrante:
Luciano Casado

Breve Bio:
Luciano Casado é arquiteto de soluções e pré-vendas na GFX Consultoria, com mais de 15 anos de experiência em desenvolvimentos e integrações SAP. Formado em Sistemas de Informação, possui MBA em Arquitetura de Software e Soluções, além de ampla vivência técnica e de negócios no ecossistema SAP.

Título da Palestra:
Joule: A Nova Queridinha da SAP

Descrição da Palestra:
A apresentação tem como objetivo explicar o que é a Joule, como ela se conecta ao universo SAP e como pode facilitar a vida dos usuários de maneira prática e direta. Com exemplos ao vivo, demonstrações técnicas e uma visão estratégica, Luciano revela o papel da Joule como assistente digital integrada às soluções SAP.

*** Link com todas as palestras ***

Apresentação e Motivação Pessoal:
Luciano inicia com uma introdução pessoal e descontraída, ressaltando sua trajetória, paixão por tecnologia e a importância da comunidade técnica para compartilhar conhecimento.

Contextualização da IA no Ambiente Corporativo:
A inteligência artificial (IA) não é mais futurismo, mas realidade no mundo corporativo. Segundo dados apresentados, mais de 72% das empresas já utilizam IA de alguma forma em 2024.

Conceito de Business AI da SAP:
A SAP introduziu o conceito de "Business AI", que se baseia em dados de negócio provenientes de suas soluções transacionais (como S/4HANA, Ariba, CX, SuccessFactors). Essa camada intermediária utiliza IA de grandes provedores (como OpenAI e Google) para oferecer valor ao cliente final, mantendo segurança de dados e foco exclusivo em contextos corporativos.

Quem é Joule:
Joule é a assistente digital (copiloto) da SAP, comparada a uma “Alexa corporativa”. Sua função é atuar dentro de cada contexto SAP (como S/4HANA, CX, etc.), trazendo agilidade e praticidade para usuários, consultores e clientes finais, com uma interface amigável e em constante evolução.

Demonstrações Práticas:
Luciano realiza várias simulações com a Joule no SAP S/4HANA Public Edition. Exemplos incluem:

  • Listagem de ordens de compra não liberadas.

  • Identificação de pedidos de compra atrasados.

  • Consulta de pedidos de venda não entregues e geração de remessas.

Em todos os casos, Joule executa as ações diretamente ou direciona o usuário aos aplicativos SAP corretos, com dados previamente filtrados.

Para Consultores e Clientes:
Consultores ganham produtividade e autonomia com Joule, que poderá futuramente consultar toda a documentação SAP e indicar soluções de configuração. Clientes terão mais agilidade para acessar informações sem intermediação da TI.

Para Desenvolvedores:
Luciano demonstra a integração da Joule com o SAP Build para desenvolvimento rápido de aplicações. Joule pode gerar scripts e lógica de programação com base em comandos em linguagem natural, acelerando o ciclo de desenvolvimento.

Futuro da Joule:
SAP planeja expandir as capacidades da Joule com os conceitos de:

  • Julie Agents: múltiplos agentes especializados que colaboram entre si.

  • Julie Studio: ambiente para customizar e monetizar funcionalidades da Joule.

  • Julie for ABAP: sugestões e geração de código ABAP automatizado.

Conclusão:
A Joule representa um novo paradigma de interação com o SAP. Vai além de uma simples interface, funcionando como mediadora inteligente entre dados, processos e pessoas. É uma transformação que impactará diretamente a produtividade, a experiência do usuário e o valor entregue pelas soluções SAP.

 

se você deseja ser notificado sobre os próximos eventos SAP Inside Track no Brasil, entre na nossa lista de interesse: Cadastre-se aqui para ser notificado

entry.title: #SITCPS2025 - 🗣️ Como Extrair o Melhor do SAP com SAP Fiori e Personalizações Clean Core
entry.content:
Nome do Palestrante

Lucas Ferreira

Breve Biografia

Lucas Ferreira é Executivo Sênior na Tachyonix, com 11 anos de experiência na SAP Brasil. Especialista em pré-vendas, S4 e BTP, ele se dedica a soluções que promovem inovação e eficiência nos processos empresariais.

Título da Palestra

Como Extrair o Melhor do SAP com SAP Fiori e Personalizações Clean Core

Descrição Breve sobre a Palestra

Nesta palestra, Lucas Ferreira demonstrou como maximizar o potencial do SAP utilizando o SAP Fiori e personalizações Clean Core. Ele apresentou métodos para criar aplicações Fiori sem a necessidade de programação, mantendo a integridade das funcionalidades padrão e proporcionando uma experiência mais ágil e eficiente para os usuários.

*** Link com todas as palestras ***

Resumo

  • Introdução ao SAP Fiori e Clean Core: A palestra iniciou com uma explicação sobre o SAP Fiori, destacando sua interface amigável e adaptável, e o conceito de Clean Core, que visa manter o núcleo do sistema SAP limpo de personalizações invasivas.

  • Benefícios das Personalizações Clean Core: Lucas enfatizou que as personalizações Clean Core permitem adaptações às necessidades específicas das empresas sem comprometer as atualizações futuras do sistema, garantindo maior estabilidade e menor custo de manutenção.

  • Criação de Aplicações Fiori sem Programação: Foi demonstrado como é possível desenvolver aplicações Fiori utilizando ferramentas de low-code/no-code, facilitando a criação de soluções personalizadas por usuários com pouco ou nenhum conhecimento em programação.

  • Integração com SAP BTP: A integração com a SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) foi abordada, mostrando como ela suporta as extensões Clean Core e permite a criação de aplicações escaláveis e seguras.

  • Casos Práticos e Resultados: Lucas compartilhou casos de sucesso onde a adoção do SAP Fiori e das personalizações Clean Core resultaram em melhorias significativas na eficiência operacional e na satisfação dos usuários finais.

  • Recomendações Finais: A palestra concluiu com recomendações para as empresas que desejam adotar o SAP Fiori e as personalizações Clean Core, enfatizando a importância de uma estratégia bem definida e do envolvimento das equipes de TI e de negócios.

se você deseja ser notificado sobre os próximos eventos SAP Inside Track no Brasil, entre na nossa lista de interesse: Cadastre-se aqui para ser notificado

entry.title: #SITCPS2025 - 🗣️ SAP Business AI: Aplicação de IA Generativa em Cenários de Negócio
entry.content:
Palestrante:
Sávio Marques

Biografia:
Profissional com mais de 20 anos de experiência em TI, especializado em soluções SAP. Atua como BTP Solution Advisor na SAP, com foco em pré-vendas, adoção e geração de demanda. É especialista em SAP Build Suite, CAP, Fiori e Integration Suite, com profundo conhecimento em aplicações de IA Generativa.

Título da Palestra:
SAP Business AI: Aplicação de IA Generativa em Cenários de Negócio

Descrição da Palestra:
Sávio Marques apresentou como a SAP está incorporando IA Generativa em suas soluções empresariais. Ele discutiu o conceito de RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) e o impacto estratégico da IA nos processos de negócio, destacando agentes inteligentes, integração com LLMs e a democratização da tecnologia.

*** Link com todas as palestras ***

Resumo Educativo

  • Conceito de SAP Business AI:
    A SAP está reformulando suas soluções para aplicar inteligência artificial de forma prática e estratégica, criando a linha "SAP Business AI" que visa integrar IA diretamente nos processos empresariais.

  • Importância dos Dados Harmonizados:
    Sávio destaca que a base da aplicação de IA está na organização dos dados transacionais. Quanto mais padronizados e enriquecidos semanticamente, melhor o desempenho das ferramentas de IA para gerar insights.

  • Agentes Inteligentes e a Revolução no SAP:
    A palestra mostra como os agentes com IA generativa estão começando a executar tarefas complexas autonomamente, como consultas de pedidos, criação de ordens de venda e interação com usuários em linguagem natural.

  • Parceria com Modelos de Linguagem (LLMs):
    A SAP firmou contratos com provedores como OpenAI, Gemini e Mistral, assegurando que os dados do cliente não serão usados para treinar os modelos, mantendo a conformidade com LGPD.

  • Integração com SAP BTP:
    O BTP é a base onde são desenvolvidas soluções personalizadas com IA. Inclui serviços de extração de dados de documentos fiscais usando IA, mesmo sem padrões definidos, e integração com ferramentas como Databricks.

  • Exemplos Práticos de Aplicações:
    Foram demonstradas aplicações de IA em SAP S/4HANA, como previsão de atraso em pedidos, compensação bancária e reconciliação de estoque. Muitas delas já estão disponíveis desde versões de 2020.

  • Orquestração e Criação de Agentes:
    Mostrou-se como criar agentes que utilizam LLMs, conectam-se com dados do SAP e executam fluxos completos de processos de forma automatizada, como criar ordens de venda com campos definidos dinamicamente.

  • Uso de Documentos Vetorizados e RAG:
    O sistema também é capaz de utilizar vetorização de documentos para fornecer respostas precisas em contexto, como recomendar palestras com base em documentos do evento.

  • Reflexão Final:
    Sávio encerra incentivando os participantes a refletirem sobre quais tarefas repetitivas em seus trabalhos podem ser otimizadas com IA, democratizando o acesso a tecnologias de ponta.

se você deseja ser notificado sobre os próximos eventos SAP Inside Track no Brasil, entre na nossa lista de interesse: Cadastre-se aqui para ser notificado

entry.title: Palestras realizadas #SITCPS2025
entry.content:
Propósito do Evento (#SITCPS2025) 

O SAP Inside Track é um evento comunitário global, organizado por membros da comunidade SAP, com o objetivo de promover o aprendizado colaborativo, networking e a troca de experiências sobre soluções SAP. Em Campinas, o evento destacou-se como uma oportunidade única para os participantes se atualizarem sobre as tendências tecnológicas e práticas recomendadas no universo SAP. Veja o blog sobre o evento.

Destaques das Palestras

O evento contou com uma programação diversificada de palestras, abordando temas como inteligência artificial, personalizações no SAP Fiori, metodologias de transformação digital, entre outros. Especialistas compartilharam insights valiosos e casos práticos, proporcionando aos participantes uma visão abrangente das possibilidades e desafios no uso das soluções SAP.

Palestra Nome do Palestrante Link

Inteligência Artificial para Consultores Funcionais@Secco Link
O fim da TAXBRARVABRA e a chegada do TaaS@fabiodezan Link
Uso de APIs na Comunicação Bancária dos Processos Financeiros no SAP@MRobalinho Link
RISE with SAP MethodologyArthur PlacidoLink
Joule: A Nova Queridinha da SAPLuciano CasadoLink
Soluções SAP para Transformação de Negócios (Signavio e LeanIX)@Rodrigo_Cordeiro Link
Como Extrair o Melhor do SAP com SAP Fiori e Personalizações Clean CoreLucas FerreiraLink
SAP Business AI: Aplicação de IA Generativa em Cenários de NegócioSávio MarquesLink
Desafios da Reforma Tributária Brasileira@thamires_k + PudenziLink

se você deseja ser notificado sobre os próximos eventos SAP Inside Track no Brasil, entre na nossa lista de interesse: Cadastre-se aqui para ser notificado

entry.title: Sydney Stammtisch Recap May '25
entry.content:
The sun didn't make an appearance at our latest Sydney Stammtisch....but plenty of Sydneysiders did.  We had a good mix of regulars and new faces.

I got some good feedback on the venue (the Keg & Brew).  There was plenty of room up on the rooftop, the pizzas hit the spot and even though it did rain a bit we stayed cosy and dry under the awning.  We will definitely be back, and this time we won't be waiting 8 years!

I'm currently sounding people out about August 14th for the next event. Let me know in the comments what you think, and whether you have any good venues to suggest.

Thanks to @jeff_t & Mausmi for additional photos.  As always I'm sorry not to have got everyone in a photo

entry.title: SAP Community Kyiv
entry.content:
Привіт, SAP-ентузіасти України! 🔥

Раді вітати Вас у нашій спільноті! Це місце, де збираються всі, хто цікавиться SAP і хоче розвивати експертизу у впровадженні SAP-рішень. Шукаєте найкращі практики, цінні поради чи просто хочете познайомитися з колегами? Ця спільнота саме для вас🙌🏻

А ви знали? У 2025 році SAP — світовий лідер корпоративного програмного забезпечення і найдорожча компанія Європи — відзначатиме 30 років діяльності в Україні, впроваджуючи інновації та підтримуючи бізнес у різних галузях. Приєднуйтесь до нас, щоб разом творити майбутнє!

Що тут відбувається? / Чому варто приєднатися?

  • Будьте в курсі локальних ініціатив, долучайтеся до розмов, ставте запитання або просто лайкайте й коментуйте дописи.
  • Беріть участь в ексклюзивних подіях — дискусіях, зустрічах та обміні знаннями, адаптованих під українські бізнес-реалії.
  • Ми створюємо комфортний простір для всіх, хто хоче познайомитися з SAP-екосистемою, отримати підтримку на початку шляху або просто розширити професійні горизонти — кожен знайде тут щось цінне для себе.

Ми страшенно раді, що ви з нами! Давайте рости й розвиватися разом! 🚀

Як приєднатися? Тут все детально описано.

 

Та мусимо попередити про важливу річ: як тільки ви закінчите реєстрацію, сайт покаже вам “Access failed”, але не лякайтеся! Варто зайти знову через 1-2 дні – і все, SAP Community Kyiv до Ваших послуг😉

 

Ми відкриті до ваших відгуків і з нетерпінням чекаємо на спілкування! Якщо ви хочете запропонувати тему майбутньої події, поділитися корисною інформацією чи ідеєю для обговорення, будь ласка, напишіть нам!

Щоб розпочати спілкування, представтеся, будь ласка! Просто натисніть кнопку "Відповісти" під цим дописом і розкажіть про:

  • Де ви працюєте?
  • Цікавий факт про себе!
  • Які теми SAP вас найбільше цікавлять?

 

Зауважте, що, як і всюди в спільноті, нам потрібно дотримуватися Правил взаємодії, а якщо виникнуть питання щодо Груп, завжди можна звернутися до розділу Допомоги.

З нетерпінням чекаємо на знайомство з усіма вами! 😊

 

 

Hey, SAP enthusiasts of Ukraine! 🔥

We're stoked to welcome you to our Community! This is where everyone into SAP hangs out and wants to develop expertise in implementing SAP solutions. Whether you are exploring best practices, looking for valuable insights, or just connecting with peers, this Community is built for you! 🙌🏻

Did you know? In 2025, SAP—the world leader in Enterprise software & Europe's most valuable company—will celebrate 30 years in Ukraine, driving innovation and supporting businesses across industries. Join us in shaping the future together!

What happens here? / Why join?

  • Stay in the loop with local initiatives, jump into conversations, ask questions, or just hit like and drop comments on posts.
  • Join exclusive events – participate in discussions, meetups, and knowledge-sharing sessions tailored for the Ukrainian business landscape.
  • We're creating a comfortable space for everyone who wants to get familiar with the SAP ecosystem, receive support at the beginning of their journey, or simply expand their professional horizons—everyone will find something valuable here.

We're super excited you're with us! Let's grow and develop together! 🚀

How to join? Everything is described in detail here.

But we must warn you about one important thing: once you complete the registration, the site will show you "Access failed," but don't worry! Just try logging in again after 1-2 days – and that's it, SAP Community Kyiv is at your service! 😉

We're open to your feedback and can't wait to hear from you! If you want to suggest a topic for a future event, share useful information, or an idea for discussion, please write to us!

To get the conversation started, we'd love for you to introduce yourself! Just hit the "Reply" button below this post and tell us about:

  • Where do you work?
  • An interesting fact about yourself! ·
  • What SAP topics are you most interested in?

Please note that, as with everything else in the Community, we all need to follow the Rules of Engagement, and in case of questions about Groups, the Help section is there to guide you.

entry.title: SAP CAP CodeJam at Hyderabad 🇮🇳 8-9 May 2025
entry.content:
We spent the day learning about SAP Cloud Application Programming Model SAP HANA Cloud SAP Fiori . 

Special thanks to @Sweekar @RavikumarJagu @Chaituyel @abhilashgampa @Kamalika_bhar 

Kudos to all the participants for taking their day out of their busy schedules and learning SAP CAP and networking all the way. 

Infosys Hyderabad - Gachibowli 

Mindset Consulting - Kondapur, Hyderabad

entry.title: 2025 Sapphire Selfies with Bluebeard
entry.content:
In an effort to not only grow my ego but also show appreciation to those attending Sapphire who take a moment to pose with me for a picture I want to collect all of them here, so please if you take a pic with me post it here as a comment and I will add all the ones I take. I’ll be sure to mention you if we exchange info and then you can reply back with any info about yourself or any additional great pics of us you may have during the week here in Orlando at SAP Sapphire 

You can also share what you are doing at Sapphire and tag others that have a session etc.  I’ll be in the SAP Learning & Certification Lab, or wandering to show floor and streets of Orlando + Epcot so shout out if you see me and let’s get a selfie!

To get us started I have me and Markus Marsch & Timo Schuette.  And I had to throw in Bobblehead Bluebeard...

 

Markus, Timo and Bluebeard selfieBluebeard and Bobblehead Bluebeard

entry.title: SAP CHOTA TALKS Hyderabad 2025 Q2 - RECAP
entry.content:
The SAP Community Hyderabad witnessed another milestone event as SAP CHOTA Talks returned for its Q2 2025 edition. The gathering brought around 125+  industry leaders, SAP experts, and technology enthusiasts  for an afternoon of knowledge sharing, networking, and collaborative growth.

 The event kicked off promptly at 3:00 PM with the traditional lamp lighting ceremony, symbolizing the illumination of knowledge and innovation that would follow. This was quickly followed by a special video presentation from MOURI Tech, setting the stage for the technological discourse ahead.

 

SAP Community Overview

Dr. Mahesh Kumar CV delivered an insightful overview of the SAP community landscape, highlighting the vibrant ecosystem that continues to thrive in Hyderabad. His presentation emphasized how community collaboration drives innovation and provides support for organizations navigating digital transformation journeys.

 

Speaker Session - GenAI: Transforming Enterprise Operations

The first speaker session, led by Venkata Chivukula, a Data Scientist at MOURI Tech, delved into the exciting intersection of SAP and Generative AI. The session explored how GenAI is poised to transform enterprise operations, offering insights into potential use cases and the impact on existing SAP landscapes. Attendees gained valuable perspectives on leveraging AI for increased efficiency and innovation.

https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1I3AY4jVMTesdck09UXl8tKu4K5oJDLqu?usp=sharing

 

Ask Anything: Interactive Q&A

The "Ask Anything" segment featured experts Arogya Gallela, Sultana Khan, Poonam Singh and Bhavani Prasad Jogu fielding questions on diverse topics ranging from AI & Analytics in SAP HCM to success factors in SAP ABAP on HANA & Customer experiences. This interactive session allowed attendees to gain clarity on specific implementation challenges and best practices.

 

Networking

The midway refreshment break proved to be more than just a time for refreshments. It transformed into a vibrant networking session where attendees exchanged business cards, discussed challenges, and formed potential collaborations.

Special Acknowledgments

A special note of gratitude goes to Sushi, CMO of Instabites, for the wonderful support and gesture in providing the exceptional catering services. The culinary experience offered by Instabites elevated the networking session and added a delightful dimension to the event, demonstrating how attention to detail enhances professional gatherings.

S/4HANA Migration Panel Discussion

A dynamic panel featuring industry experts Gupta Kandula, Immanuel Kolagani, Venkat tackled one of the most pressing topics in the SAP ecosystem today - S/4HANA migration strategies. The discussion centred around maximizing ROI on SAP ECC investments during migration, with panellists sharing valuable insights from their own implementation experiences.

Reskilling in the Age of S/4HANA

Post-break, Dr. Mahesh Kumar CV returned to the stage, this time wearing his hat as Thought Leader. His session on reskilling needs in the era of S/4HANA, BTP, BDC, and AI provided actionable guidance for professionals looking to stay relevant in the rapidly evolving SAP technology landscape with learning Pathways.

 

https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1I3AY4jVMTesdck09UXl8tKu4K5oJDLqu?usp=sharing

The event concluded with a lucky draw, tokens of appreciation for speakers, and a vote of thanks to the core team. As attendees departed, the conversations continued, with many already looking forward to the next edition of SAP CHOTA Talks Q3.

 

@Ruthvik_Chowdary @KatherineKenneally @marco_v_santos @lauraschmitz

entry.title: SAP CHOTA Talks Q2 2025: A Resounding Success and a Glimpse into the Future of SAP in Hyderabad!
entry.content:
Hyderabad, the city of pearls and burgeoning tech hub, recently played host to a truly exceptional event: SAP CHOTA Talks Q2 2025. This quarterly gathering, already a staple in the SAP community calendar, outdid itself this time around, bringing together an impressive confluence of industry leaders, seasoned experts, and enthusiastic tech aficionados. As an attendee, I can confidently say it was an experience that left me both inspired and incredibly well-informed.

The air buzzed with anticipation as Dr. Mahesh Kumar CV kicked off the event with a visionary opening address. His emphasis on the critical role of community collaboration in driving innovation resonated deeply, setting the perfect tone for a day filled with insightful discussions and knowledge sharing. It was a powerful reminder that in the rapidly evolving world of SAP, collective intelligence and shared experiences are paramount.

One of the undeniable highlights was the session led by Venkata Chivukula from MOURI Tech, who masterfully explored the integration of Generative AI in SAP. This wasn't just a theoretical deep dive; Venkata showcased real-world applications, painting a vivid picture of the transformative power of AI in enterprise operations. It was fascinating to see how Generative AI is moving beyond buzzwords and offering tangible solutions that can truly revolutionize how businesses operate.

"Ask Anything" segment was a whirlwind of practical wisdom. With a panel featuring Arogya Gallela, Sultana Khan, Poonam Singh, and Bhavani Prasad Jogu, participants had the invaluable opportunity to gain direct insights into pressing topics like SAP HCM, ABAP on HANA, and customer-centric solutions. The candid responses and practical advice shared by these experts were truly invaluable, providing actionable takeaways for everyone in attendance.

Another session that truly resonated was the thought-provoking panel discussion on navigating the migration to S/4HANA, featuring Gupta Kandula, Immanuel Kolagani, and Venkat. For many businesses, the S/4HANA migration is a significant undertaking, and this panel offered a wealth of actionable strategies and real-world experiences. Their insights provided a clear roadmap for organizations undergoing this critical transformation, alleviating many common concerns and offering a sense of direction.

Dr. Mahesh Kumar CV, led a crucial session on reskilling in the S/4HANA era. In an ever-evolving technological landscape, the need for continuous learning and adaptation is paramount. Dr. Mahesh provided clear learning pathways, equipping professionals with the knowledge and resources to stay ahead of the curve in this fast-paced environment. It was a proactive approach to skill development that was much appreciated by the audience.

Beyond the insightful sessions, the event was made even more memorable by a delightful culinary experience from "Insta Bites." Instabites Sushi, courtesy of Gowtham Chagarlamudi, proved to be a fantastic addition, enhancing both the networking opportunities and the overall enjoyment of the day. Good food always fosters good conversations, and this was certainly the case at SAP CHOTA Talks Q2.

As the event drew to a close, there was a palpable sense of excitement and satisfaction in the room. A big thank you is due to the entire SAP community for their active participation, to our esteemed speakers for their invaluable contributions, and to MOURI Tech for their collaborative spirit. Their dedication truly made this event a resounding success.

The inspiring conversations and renewed connections forged at SAP CHOTA Talks Q2 2025 have left us all eagerly anticipating the upcoming Q3 edition. If this quarter's event is any indication, the future of SAP in Hyderabad is bright, driven by a vibrant community dedicated to innovation and collaboration. I, for one, am already looking forward to more impactful discussions and collaborations in the next edition!

For more detailed information, please refer to the blog by Dr. Mahesh: https://community.sap.com/t5/hyderabad-blog-posts/sap-chota-talks-hyderabad-2025-q2-recap/ba-p/14106053

@MaheshCV @Ruthvik_Chowdary

entry.title: Blog It Forward - Marco Büscher 2025 Edition
entry.content:
Thank you for the opportunity to give a friendly introduction in the form of the BIF format. My name is Marco Büscher, and I am a passionate SAP consultant with a deep enthusiasm for all things Man at the SAP World Tour 2010 in HannoverSAP technologies. Even when I immersed myself in the world of enterprise software in the field of MM in 2009, I was fascinated by the possibilities that SAP offers to optimize business processes and make companies more efficient.

SAP training material

I have now spent the last 15+ years learning MM, PP, ABAP, SD, CO, and FI (on the stack on the left, the training documents for sales and controlling are still missing, but these have now been digitized in the SAP Learning HUB to save paper and costs). Each of these modules not only provided me with technical know-how but also taught me the importance of understanding the needs and challenges of users.  But, I think you can all agree with me on one fact: having a lot of good capabilities on PowerPoint slides explaining to you what could happen is nice but will not do the job, and that's what we're looking for, especially when it comes to transformation. I am often asked, What are you doing? And I say, we are NTT DATA, one of the biggest consulting companies in the world. Our mission statement is NTT DATA uses information technology to create new paradigms and value that help contribute to a more affluent and harmonious society. In simpler terms, NTT DATA aims to leverage technology to improve society and create value for its clients and the world. And that is, for me, the way, and I go with SAP…  So we have three tasks. First, we will do our utmost to serve our customers. Second, we will meet the challenges of our work with an active and positive spirit. Third, we will create a lively and vibrant company. So, SAP was already working in real-time in the 70s, which meant that SAP systems were able to process and deliver data in real-time, which was an incredible step forward and only added to my fascination with SAP. We consultants, for example, look at a seven-year timeframe because we are concerned with short-term developments at SAP, and we look at the seven-year period, and we expect seven to nineteen years and more in terms of skills, development, and overall planning. I believe that real solutions and KI are not only about code and technical skills but also about empathy and the ability to communicate with and lead people. So what I am really after is getting the full picture of the Business and acting better and getting better effects... I enjoy working in a dynamic environment where I am constantly challenged to find new solutions and collaborate with different teams. In every new challenge, be it a complex project or a new customer contact, I see an opportunity to learn and grow.  My goal is to be there for my customers, not only as a consultant or developer but also as a partner at NTT DATA — someone who understands them and helps them achieve their goals.

Years ago, we learned about SAP innovations at CeBIT in Hanover, and I sometimes go there. There were new features and innovations to see, as well as artifacts from the history of SAP development. Among other things, an illustration of one of the punch cards that SAP had created for ICI from the SAP timeline. Punched cards are a historical medium for data storage and processing. They usually consist of thick cardboard or plastic cards with holes in certain positions. These holes represent binary data (0 and 1) and made it possible to store information such as machine code or data.

People at the SAP stand at Cebit 2010 in HannoverMotivation at the SAP stand at Cebit 2010 in HannoverSAP Cargo Scenario with SAP HANA

Man on one of the first bikes with Bluetooth at SAP

 

 

 

 

 

 

Man in front of the SAP Timeline8-bit system for mainframe computers and punched cards

SAP Codejam´s

Participants SAP CodeJam 2023 in Heilbronn

From my point of view in a role as an SAP consultant and developer, I always strive to actively share knowledge and foster a community of SAP enthusiasts. For this reason, I am committed to organizing SAP CodeJams throughout Germany, for example, to see what the future holds for us because if you make the mistake, which is quite often made, that you just look at what the operations hold for you today and what you plan to have today, you always prepare for the business of yesterday. Participants SAP CodeJam 2019 in Lingen(EMS)These events not only offer an excellent platform to enter into dialogue with colleagues and interested developers, but also the opportunity to learn together and develop innovative ideas. For me, this is not only about sharing knowledge but also about strengthening the team spirit and creating a creative space where we can work together on agile solutions to complex challenges. Because every Company has the same problem: limited recources of people so with that, how do we do it in practical terms?

 

SAP Meetup´s

I also really enjoy bringing people together—whether at informal meetings such as regulars' tables or in small groups—where they can present their projects, exchange ideas, and inspire each other through open and meaningful discussions.Speaker at an SAP regulars' table in Lingen 2017

 

 

SAP Education

Wall in the SAP building Ratingen 2018

So what has changed over time is as well again from a SAP Consultants Point of view, is learning and certifying in SAP modules. It used to be a very different experience. In Ratingen i.e. a popular training location, professionals gathered in classrooms to delve deeply into the complex facets of SAP. The atmosphere was characterized by a mixture of curiosity and engagement, while trainers delved deep into the content and encouraged participants to actively participate.

Questions were discussed, solutions debated and practical exercises carried out in front of the large screens and whiteboards. It was a time when personal interaction and direct exchange with trainers and other participants enriched the learning environment and deepened understanding of the software. The diversity of perspectives and experiences of those present helped to provide a broader picture of the SAP world. So what i am really after is getting the full picture, getting a full synchronization having better picture and acting better to develop better solutions. This type of learning not only created knowledge but also a network of contacts that lasted beyond the training. With the shift to the digital world, the framework conditions may have changed, but the desire to continuously develop and learn remains regardless of the format.

The memories of these intensive training days, even though they were conducted by an SAP training partner, are characterized by a sense of comRoom allocation plan for the Zerti in the SAP building Ratingen 2018munity, which was not only about individual efforts but also about teamwork and mutual support. Just let me also stress one point: the opportunity to ask personal questions directly to experienced trainers or colleagues and to learn in a dynamic group fostered a deep understanding of the subject was profitable for all, and that's the point where also the SAP Community came in. Here we can have a community that is open-minded to get in communication with others. Even though virtual training has now taken precedence, the desire for these personal experiences and face-to-face exchanges remains alive, as they formed a valuable foundation for many in their professional development.

 

Visit to Walldorf

The SAP Logo and spring fountain in WalldorfAnother highlight of my trip was undoubtedly the visit to the SAP headquarters in Walldorf. As I entered the modern buildings, I immediately felt the electrifying atmosphere that surrounded the place. Every element seemed to reflect the innovative power that SAP is known for. Talking to Svea Becker, the SAP Community Advocate, the passion and vision behind SAP's work became even clearer to me.

I was particularly impressed by the ongoing renovation work on the buildings, which are around 30 years old. This measure shows SAP's ambition to continuously renew itself and remain attractive to young talent. It is not just a question of aesthetic appearance, but also a sign of appreciation for the creative minds Svea Becker, SAP Advocate at SAP and me in Walldorf who will be working in these modern premises. The company wants to show that there is a place for innovation and fresh ideas - and this is reflected in the modern architecture that I was able to admire. 

Another fascinating part of my visit was the walk through the Zen garden. This tranquil oasis not only provided a contrast to the hustle and bustle of the offices, but also symbolized the open corporate culture that SAP stands for. In this harmonious setting, Svea and I talked about the various initiatives SAP has launched to strengthen the community. At this place I can feel that SAP works for Customers and Partners and not against them... The People have a lot of engagement for their Job... 

Svea shed light on the challenges facing the community such as the fast-moving technology landscape and the need to constantly adapt. Her stories about the strategies SAP is pursuing to overcome these hurMan in front of the SAP International Training Center Walldorfdles were both insightful and inspiring. She conveyed a sense of confidence that, despite the difficulties, collaboration and sharing within the community can lead to real progress. Finally, we had the opportunity to take a look at the international training center. The visit to Walldorf will remain in my memory not only as a professional highlight but also as a profound experience that shaped my perspective on the importance of community and collaboration in the technology industry. It was a trip that not only opened up new perspectives for me but also highlighted the unmistakable power of networking and shared learning.

 

 

Fun facts about me

Golf ball and golf club

Golfers on tee 1Taking part in golf not only offers the opportunity to relax and network but also teaches a variety of skills that are of great importance in the professional world. Golf is a game of strategy and strategic thinking. Every shot requires careful consideration—from choosing the right club to assessing the course conditions. These skills are equally important in the business world, where decisions often have far-reaching consequences and well-thought-out strategies can make the difference between success and failure.

In addition, golf is a school of patience. Success on the course is rarely immediate; rather, it requires repeated practice and a willingness to learn from setbacks. This perseverance, coupled with the ability to take the long view, is invaluable in many professions, especially those involving long-term projects or complex tasks.

"How to Play Golf" | A Classic Mickey Cartoon

Another important aspect of the game of golf is the value of fair play. Etiquette and respectful interaction on the golf course foster a sense of integrity that is also important in the business world. Fairness and recognizing the contributions of others are critical to building trust and creating a positive team dynamic.

 

 

 

Describe in a short sentence: "What needs does your SAP solution meet?

Our SAP solutions meet the need for seamlessly integrated, automated, and flexible business processes that enable real-time data analysis and improve efficiency and decision-making in organizations.

 

What 5 things do you really want to achieve in life?

Family and relationships: Build and maintain strong, loving relationships with family and friends. Travel and cultural experiences: Explore the world, learn about different cultures, and have unforgettable travel experiences. Personal development: Constant learning and personal development through education, new hobbies, and self-reflection. Financial independence: Build a secure financial foundation to minimize future uncertainty. Making a positive impact: acting in the community to help others and bring about positive social change.

 

What do you envision or predict for the future of your topic? (Mobile, cloud, etc.—be creative, be brave, be entertaining.)

Human Interface 2.0: Imagine a world where your thoughts are transformed directly into the cloud to share information or decisions in real-time. Brain-computer interfaces could act as the new smartphone—simple, and the data flows!

Emotional AI assistants: Our mobile devices could develop a higher level of emotional understanding and support us as personal life coaches. They would be able to recognize our mood and make appropriate suggestions for activities, music, or conversations to promote our well-being.

 

If your work gave you a whole day every week to do whatever you feel like doing, what would it be?

If I had a whole day every week to do whatever I wanted, I would spend it playing golf. I love being outdoors, and there’s something special about the fresh air on the course. I’d focus on my swing, strategize my next moves, and enjoy the game. It’s also a great way to hang out with friends or meet new people. Whether I’m practicing my drives, working on my putting, or just soaking in the beautiful scenery, a day of golf would be relaxing and fun!

 

What was the funniest project you have ever participated in, and why?

We were called to a two-week project, and during this time the project manager had taken vacation.

 

Completion

If you find yourself in one or the other situation, please leave a Kudo... 🙂

I would like to pass the blog on to my colleague @Hilal_Kurt from Turkey, who has just become an SAP Champion after many years of working with the SAP community. I am sure she has many interesting things to share with us.

entry.title: SAP Communities: SAP Inside Track Porto 2025 web page
entry.content:
We have created a web page with all the information about the great SAP Inside Track Porto 2025 event, which we are organizing for September 27, 2025, in Vila Nova de Gaia.

The page brings together information about the event's location and how to get there using the various means of transport available, as well as who the sponsors are, who the organization team is, and who the volunteers are. In the future, we will post the lectures there.

We are also planning to have a SAPCodeJam the day before the event. You can find links to tickets, links to propose a lecture, and to be one of the speakers... The SAP community in Portugal is to be congratulated.

The page is wonderful, functional, and contextualized. What can I say about the Porto tiles matching the event image?

Great ideas happen when we are involved in a purpose. Register for the event and join the SAP Community in Portugal.

sitporto.com

entry.title: SAP Communities: SAP Inside Track Brazil page
entry.content:
The SAP Brazil community has created a page to publicize all SAP Inside Track Brazil events. For the year 2025, you can find the event schedule, links to tickets, and other information. Visit the SAP Brazil community page and stay updated with events in your city.

Visit: https://sitbrazil.com/

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite CodeJam roadshow in India
entry.content:
¡Hola SAP Developers!

I've got some exciting news to share with you. We are organising an SAP Integration Suite CodeJam roadshow in India!

I understand that in July, it is still season for the king of fruits in India (🥭 mango, obviously), and my colleague @ajay_soreng and I ( @ajmaradiaga ) will go on the hunt for the best mango variety in India... while we do a few CodeJams on our roadshow 😋.  We will have three stops on the roadshow from July 14th to 18th, 2025, doing a total of four CodeJams, one in Mumbai, two in Hyderabad, and one more in Bengaluru :-).

 

Connecting systems and services using SAP Integration Suite

This SAP CodeJam instance will be about SAP Integration Suite and how we can use it to connect systems and services in our landscape.

Event-driven integration with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh

This SAP CodeJam instance will be about event-driven architectures focusing on SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh. During the CodeJam, we will cover different technologies/tools: SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh, SAP Cloud Integration, SAP Business Application Studio, CloudEvents, and Node.js.

SAP CodeJam events part of the roadshow (Register Now!)

Below is a listing of the SAP CodeJam events on the roadshow schedule:

Dates

CodeJam topic

Location

Registration

14 July

Connecting systems and services using SAP Integration Suite

Mumbai 🇮🇳

Link

15 July

Event-driven integrations with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh #1

Hyderabad 🇮🇳

Link

16 July

Event-driven integrations with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh #2

Hyderabad 🇮🇳

Link

18 July

Connecting systems and services using SAP Integration Suite

Bengaluru 🇮🇳

Link

Make sure to register to secure your place. It is likely that the places available will go fast. See you all somewhere in India on the roadshow!

entry.title: Project Glow: New Design Coming to SAP Community on Monday, June 2
entry.content:
On June 2, you’ll notice design improvements on SAP Community, as the company’s Project Glow redesign rolls out to our platform.

Project Glow extends across all SAP web properties, and you can already experience the new design’s look and feel for yourself on sites such as SAP Support Portal. SAP Community will become the latest site to benefit from Project Glow, a company-wide effort to enhance usability, comply with the latest accessibility standards, and implement a cohesive and updated look and feel to customer facing digital touchpoints. 

Elements of the redesign include the introduction of 72 font (SAP’s typeface), new iconography (and removal of pictograms), and a lighter aesthetic and softer shapes.

This mock-up should give you some idea of what to expect when the changes go into effect on June 2:

While SAP did not develop Project Glow specifically for SAP Community, the new design will address some of the common concerns that we’ve heard from you. For example, headers will become smaller across the platform, and whitespace will be reduced. In addition, the “Recommendations” widget will disappear from questions and the “Labels” widget will disappear from blog posts.

We don’t expect Project Glow to impact members on June 2, but if you are working on blog posts, long questions, or detailed answers on this date, we recommend that you save drafts periodically offline, in case you need to log in again as the redesign rolls out across community servers.

Design improvements won’t end with the introduction of Project Glow. We’ll soon add a one-column layout (to make it easier to skim new posts) and add support for dark theme. We’ll announce these changes in What’s New as soon as they are available.

entry.title: Інноваційні новини на SAP Sapphire
entry.content:
SAP оголосила революційні AI-інновації на конференції Sapphire 2025🔥

На щорічній конференції SAP Sapphire компанія представила лінійку нововведень, які кардинально змінюють підхід до впровадження штучного інтелекту в бізнесі. Ключові оголошення охоплюють нові партнерства, розширення можливостей AI-асистента Joule та вдосконалення SAP Business Technology Platform.

AI Co-Innovation Program: партнерство SAP та AWS

SAP SE та AWS запустили нову програму AI Co-Innovation Program для створення генеративних ШІ-додатків, що вирішують бізнес-завдання у реальному часі. Програма об'єднує корпоративні технології SAP з генеративними послугами ШІ від AWS, включаючи Amazon Bedrock та великі мовні моделі Amazon Nova і Anthropic Claude.

Перші партнери програми — Accenture та Deloitte — вже працюють над практичними рішеннями: створенням стійкості активів до стихійних лих та фінансовими рішеннями для сфери охорони здоров'я.

Нові партнерства з Perplexity та Palantir

Партнерство з Perplexity розширює можливості AI-асистента Joule, дозволяючи йому використовувати структуровані та неструктуровані дані для миттєвих відповідей у форматі діаграм та графіків на основі реальних бізнес-даних.

Партнерство з Palantir підтримує хмарну трансформацію клієнтів з високими вимогами до безпеки, включно з урядовими структурами США, створюючи уніфіковану дата-архітектуру через інтеграцію з SAP Business Data Cloud.

Еволюція Joule: від асистента до екосистеми агентів

Joule значно розширив свої можливості — тепер він доступний практично всюди, включає проактивну панель дій на базі WalkMe та керує мережею інтелектуальних агентів, які працюють автономно у різних бізнес-напрямках.

Нововведення SAP Business Technology Platform

Joule Studio — нова функція в SAP Build для створення власних агентів ШІ та навичок Joule з використанням low-code та no-code підходів. Офіційний реліз заплановано на червень 2025 року.

AI Foundation — операційна система для Business AI, що служить єдиною точкою входу для створення та запуску AI-рішень у великих масштабах. Новий оптимізатор підказок скорочує час роботи з днів до хвилин.

Оновлений SAP Integration Suite з новими AI-можливостями для інтеграції локальних та хмарних процесів, включаючи прогнози API-трафіку та адаптер для підключення власних мовних моделей.

Розширена інтеграція з SAP Business Data Cloud — з другої половини 2025 року розробники зможуть використовувати SAP Build для прямого доступу до продуктів даних.

Практичне значення для бізнесу

Ці інновації роблять штучний інтелект доступним для середніх та великих підприємств, дозволяючи створювати власних AI-агентів без глибоких технічних знань, інтегрувати ШІ в існуючі процеси та отримувати до 35% швидшу окупність інвестицій завдяки новим рішенням для міграції в хмару.

Готові стати частиною революції Business AI? Ознайомтеся з повним Посібником з інновацій SAP Sapphire 2025 та поділіться своїми думками: які з цих інновацій найбільше вплинуть на вашу галузь?

Для отримання додаткової інформації про AWS SAP AI Co-Innovation Program відвідайте aws.amazon.com/sap/ai 

А про всі новини SAP читайте тут.🚀

entry.title: Зустрічаємося на головній події фінансових директорів України
entry.content:
Ексклюзивна знижка на Ukrainian CFO Forum від партнера SAP - Business Evolution 

Запрошуємо вас на головну подію року для фінансових директорів із ексклюзивною знижкою 20% та можливістю зустрітися з командою SAP Ukraine.

Форум Фінансових Директорів України – головна щорічна подія у сфері корпоративних фінансів, що з 1997 року об’єднує C-level керівників провідних компаній для обміну досвідом, аналізу викликів і формування стратегій сталого розвитку. Єдиний в Україні форум такого масштабу за глибиною експертизи й рівнем учасників.

Де?

  • Київ та онлайн

Коли?

  • Brave CFO Gathering — 6 червня 2025
  • Ukrainian CFO Forum — 23–24 жовтня 2025
    Більше про події тут.

Серед спікерів — наш партнер Олексюк Андрій, CCO Business Evolution. Вже 6 червня він поділиться практичним досвідом у виступі на тему: «Нові технології оптимізації ключових бізнес-процесів: співпраця фінансового та ІТ-директора».

🎯Як отримати знижку 20%

  • Використайте промокод BraveBE при реєстрації  
  • Знижка поширюється на всі типи квитків
  • Промокод діє до 3 червня 2025 року

Побачимось на форумі😉

entry.title: My Sapphire Orlando 2025 recap from an SAP Community point of view
entry.content:
Hi everybody!  Mr. bluebeard here and I wanted to give you a recap of my experience at this year's SAP Sapphire 2025 event in Orlando, Florida. 

The Show Floor

When I got to Orlando, it was a lot like coming home because I've been to several Sapphires but there's nothing like that feeling when you approach the Orange County Convention Center and you know that you're about to go through an amazing entranceway and see a beautiful show floor.  Well, that's what happened.  The SAP team designed an amazing show entrance that was very photo worthy.  The show floor had lots of vendor and partner booths to the left, and a keynote stage auditorium to the right as well as a broadcast studio for virtual attendee presentations.  Along the sides we several large and small theaters for dedicated deep dive presentations, and a meeting center in the back.  There were several areas to relax and take phone calls as well, but most amazing to me was the center hallway of the show floor.

entrance before escalator down show floor main walkway

The center hallway started off with the SAP Experience Center, which I'm part of in Team SIX (Solution, Industries and Experience), the SIX team is responsible for designing and giving people a great experience going through all the innovations that SAP software is enabling. For this Experience Center you queued up outside like you were waiting at an amusement park and then you enter and you are informed about the persona journey that you're about to partake in, much like you would at an SAP Experience Center at an SAP office.  You pick a role like CEO, CPO, CHRO, then you get a AirTag that syncs to an Android or an iPhone so you can browse all the learnings on your mobile if you didn't have time to complete everything in the Experience Center.  They brought in a industrial injection machine, a warehouse robot set up to show how you could repair that equipment if it broke using AI. Once you get done in the experience center and you exit the enclosed area you're in the center hallway again.  You see a lot of beautiful booths with experts from BTP, Applications and a lot of people that are just having conversations with our customers and partners about SAP technology.

experience center experience center guided tourairtag delivery robot arm

Then when you reach the back edge of the hallway before you get to the meeting event center that's where you see the great Learning and Certification Lab. We had a 25 person auditorium theater built into it, several different touch panels where you could choose to take a picture of yourself and have AI generate a graphic image of you tied to your user name. After completing quizzes your image & name were listed on a leaderboard for all to see.  But also as you tapped your badge at the monitors and completed knowledge exercises you accumulated learning power and the tree of learning would glow brighter the more you learned. 

Andre Bechtold preso in Lab leaderboardLearning and Certification Lab Sitting area outside labtree of learning

Now onto what I did while in the space.  In the theater at the back of the Learning and Certification Lab is where myself and one of our SAP mentors @DanielleLarocca, as well as @anita_riegel from team SIX all got together and presented in a panel format "How to Harness the Power of the SAP Community".  It was a 25 minute chat to explain to people what the SAP community is and how they can benefit from it because it's their community.  Everyone who was sitting in that room was a member of the community just by being there.  Maybe they don't use the website, so we directed them to community.sap.com and how they can use those resources once signed up, but also being present at Sapphire, SAP TechEd, SAP Inside Tracks, Meetups and attending things like an an SAP CodeJam allow for community interaction in person, around the world.

 

Also I just met and talked with people outside of the SAP learning certification lab when I wasn't in a meeting.  Speaking of meetings, a lot of my time was spent working with people that are part of the SAP Mentors and Champions programs, enjoying conversations with them about what we can do to make our community experiences better.  They met with SAP executives and leaders within several SAP organizations and expressed direct feedback, so it was very nice to be a part of those meetings along with @StephanieMarley 

Then I went to a meeting with the SAP AppHaus team, and learned more about their network of SAP AppHaus locations and what they're doing with the partner network to expand the reach of the AppHaus methodology and how we can host things like a CodeJam, or do other customer events inside of these AppHaus locations which are optimized to take people through the SAP AppHaus methodology for solving their business problems.

AppHaus meeting

My Favorite Moment

I met a lot of new friends as well at after hours events like the AWS Networking Session. One was a gentleman from Mexico, Adrian, who works for a lighting industry company.   It was honestly my favorite moment because I got to hear his story about what it was like to attend Sapphire in Philadelphia (where i live) in 1996 as well as SAP TechEd that year, so really cool stories comparing not only those early events but the changes to the software we are presenting now that his company utilizes.  Adrian has been around for a long time and he has seen the change of SAP software as well as the differences between on premise and cloud, and now the integration of AI and how businesses utilize that software. 

The Keynotes

The keynotes were great from a high level. I attended all the keynote sessions at the keynote theater, which is a large space on the show floor that was full, even into the stadium seating outside.  Seeing all the great presentations with the things that we're doing from all the executives was awesome, but seeing a friend of mine, Abishek, from the old SAP mobile team who is now moved through his career up to the point where he's working for NBC Universal and was one of the customers on stage presenting how they are using all these great things was so fun and rewarding to see. 

A few of the announcements from the keynotes:

  • Intelligent Apps for the SAP Business Data Cloud
  • Joule AI assistant availability
  • Perplexity partnership
  • AI Foundation
  • many more.... 

keynote selfie Thomas Saueressig at keynoteoverflow seating

 

Celebration Night

Then we had a great event at Disney's Epcot Center Park where SAP entertained everyone who came to Sapphire by renting out the entire park.  We had two opportunities to listen to some music from the concert artist, The Zac Brown Band, and just if you wanted to ride rides or enter into any of the different country themed sections of Epcot Park you could do that.  There were several places to grab food, snacks, drinks, and just talk to all your friends and colleagues from SAP.   It was a nice night, but a little humid as Florida tends to be. I made it halfway around the world...

Epcot entrance Zac Brown

Epcot at night

   

In Summary

Finally flying home after a long week of meetings and having great interactions with all the people on the show floor reminds me it is such an honor to get to do what I do.  As I like to say, I have a "get to job".  I get to do this!  And I enjoyed reflecting by looking at the tons of pictures that I took, especially for the Selfies with bluebeard post, Well over 30 people commented on my beard which was so great cause I used that as an ice breaker to get a pic.  I know @moshenaveh has asked other people attending Sapphire to write up their experiences from this year's Sapphire and I look forward to reading those recaps. 

It was a certifiably good time.  Hope to see you at the next event.

who, what, where?!?

entry.title: SAP Community Team Spotlight: Anne Petteroe - May 2025 Edition
entry.content:
You might have seen our iterations of recognizing active authors (one / two / three / four / five / six)  in SAP Community. I am thrilled to share a new edition of this initiative. Now we also want to introduce the team behind the SAP Community platform. We start of with @AnnePetteroe with you which I interviewed recently. Read more about how she got started and what her day to day looks like behind the scenes of the SAP Community. 

Marco Santos (MS): Hi Anne! Thank you so much for joining me today and for making time for this interview! How did you first get involved with the SAP Community?

Anne Kathrine Petteroe: Happy to be here. That’s a bit of a long story! I first discovered the SAP Community back in 2005, when I was preparing for my certification as an ABAP developer. I was  living in Germany at the time and came across the community forums while looking for answers attending an SAP training  I wasn’t very active then, I was more of a reader, but the community was incredibly helpful during my training and  exam preparation.

Then, a few years later, Twitter became popular and opened up new ways to engage more directly with other community members. I think it was around 2007 or 2008 when things really started to shift. I was chatting with some folks from the community on Twitter, and Craig Cmehil mentioned a community team Christmas party in Walldorf. He invited everyone to come along.

At the time, I was living in Cologne, as was Oliver Kohl, though we had never met in person. We connected on Twitter and decided to drive to Walldorf together for the event. I remember it was a dark December evening. I was standing at a petrol station in Cologne waiting for this stranger, Oliver, to pick me up for a road trip across Germany. Sounds crazy, right?

But it worked out perfectly! We had a great time at the party, and that night I met many of the core community team members including Craig for the first time . That event was a turning point. It catapulted me from being a quiet listener to becoming an engaged contributor in the community.

MS: That’s such a unique way to get involved! Everyone has their own entry point, but yours is definitely one of the more memorable ones.

Anne Kathrine Petteroe: Yes, it really was.

MS: Maybe you can tell us a bit more about your current role on the SAP Community team. What does a typical day look like for you?

Anne Kathrine Petteroe: Sure! Today, I work as one of the moderators and advocates for the SAP Community. That means I help moderate content, review blog posts, answer questions, and support new community members by guiding them as they find their way around the platform. I  like to stay engaged, not just on the SAP Community platform itself, but on other  platforms like LinkedIn or Bluesky, wherever the community is having conversations.

Honestly, though, I wouldn’t say there’s a typical day. Every day is different because, at the end of the day, I work with people and people shape what my day will look like. The one constant is that I always start my morning by checking in on the community platform: I look for any new questions, reported content, or anything that needs immediate attention.  

MS: Sounds like a very dynamic (and busy!) routine.

Anne Kathrine Petteroe: Yes, it’s definitely busy! But also flexible, which I love. No two days look the same. Even though it’s an online platform, I still feel like I’m working with people every day. We also manage support inboxes, so there’s a lot of behind-the-scenes help we provide to community members as well. And that’s what I find really rewarding, feeling like we’re genuinely making a difference for others.

MS: Absolutely, it must be very fulfilling. Let’s jump to the next question. What’s one part of the community experience that you’re especially proud of contributing to?

Anne Kathrine Petteroe: One of the things I’m most proud of so far this year is the launch of the Community Feedback List.

One of my personal goals this year is to strengthen the feedback loop between the community and SAP. . I’ve experienced the community from both sides: both as a passionate member and now as part of the SAP team. That gives me a unique perspective, and I really want to use that to help build trust and transparency.

Something I often hear on LinkedIn is that people want to “bring back the community magic” or “the spirit of the community.” I always ask: What exactly does that mean to you? What does that look like in action? Because I truly want to understand and help make it happen.

I don’t think the “magic” is only about the platform; it’s as much about the people and the interactions. The  feedback list, to me, is a first step in re-establishing that connection, re-establishing the conversations.

One of the most important aspects of the community to me, was always the respect we have for each other. We never all agreed on everything, but we always respected each other’s viewpoints and differences, agreed to disagree and still left the room as community friends/colleagues. The community always offered so many opportunities to learn something new, and to grow as human being as well in the process.

MS: Yeah, I definitely agree. It’s a solid step in the right direction. Since we’re already speaking about community magic and spirit: can you share a memorable moment from your time working with the community?

Anne Kathrine Petteroe: There’s definitely one moment that stands out. In 2018, I was part of the TechEd keynote demo team in Barcelona, and that was  to this day, the biggest career highlight for me. I was invited to do the ABAP Cloud demo, which was such an honor.

Another aspect that made it memorable was that we were three women presenting the technical demos on stage. That felt significant and empowering.

And the best part? I managed to keep it a total secret beforehand, no one knew! So yes, that’s definitely my top community memory. There are others, of course, but that moment was truly incredible.

MS: That’s amazing. What a big step! That must’ve been such a rewarding experience.

Anne Kathrine Petteroe: Absolutely. It really was.

MS: Let’s go back to your day-to-day work with the community. How do you stay in touch with community needs and feedback?

Anne Kathrine Petteroe: That’s a great question, because honestly, it involves being everywhere and anywhere. We’ve set up structure by asking community members to post platform-related questions or bugs on the “Questions About SAP Web Sites” board or in the Coffee Corner group discussion. I monitor those closely. But I also keep an eye on all areas of the community. Sometimes feedback pops up in a comment under someone’s blog post or discussion, so it’s important to be present and observant across the different platforms .

We’re also active on social media like LinkedIn, BlueSky, and I try to follow as many conversations as I can. People also reach out via private messages, Teams, or other channels, and I make sure feedback from those sources is captured, too.

MS: That’s so great to hear! What do you enjoy most about working on the SAP Community?

Anne Kathrine Petteroe: What I enjoy most is the combination of people and technology. I’ve always been close to SAP tech, I still have ABAP stickers on my laptop! But what makes it truly rewarding is that we’re genuinely making a difference in people’s workday. I remember how much I relied on the community myself when I was delivering projects. Sometimes, I don’t think I could’ve done it without it. And knowing we’re still providing that value for others today is deeply motivating.

Also, the connections and friendships I’ve built over the years mean a lot. . Some of my closest professional friendships started here.  These are real, lasting friendships, and that’s something truly special.

MS: What’s one thing you’d love to see more in the community moving forward?

Anne Kathrine Petteroe: I’d love to see more human connection again: those genuine people-to-people moments that make the community feel alive. That “magic” isn’t just about the platform, it’s about the conversations, the shared experiences, the events where we meet, laugh, and grow together. I miss that spirit of being present and available as people, not just users. We all have a role to play in bringing that energy back, and I hope we can keep building those connections that truly make the community worthwhile.

MS: Before we finish—anything else you’d like to share with the community? Any thoughts we didn’t touch on?

Anne Kathrine Petteroe: I think the SAP community is so much more than just the platform or the tech. It’s about that blend of knowledge, education, and real human connections. The magic or spirit that people talk about comes from the space between our jobs and the relationships we build, those are what make it all worthwhile.

MS: Absolutely, it’s the connections that really make the community what it is.

Anne Kathrine Petteroe: Exactly. It’s those in-between moments that define everything.

MS: Well, thank you so much for your time today, Anne. It’s been a pleasure speaking with you!

Anne Kathrine Petteroe: Thank you, Marco! It was a pleasure!
